concerns.text: The Edge Cases section contains a literal '[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]' warning string: 'The system must log a `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` warning and halt'. This violates the kickback requirement that a converged spec must have NO surviving '[NEEDS CLARIFICATION: ...]' markers. The spec must resolve this by defining the actual behavior (e.g., 'halt with a specific error code') or removing the placeholder text.

FR-007 mandates a sensitivity analysis sweeping thresholds at counts {1, 2, 3}. The idea explicitly asks to test independence 'even when controlling for cognitive dissonance derived from a separate set of non-AI-specific ethical attitudes'. The spec defines the control as 'General Trait Empathy' (US-003, FR-002) but does not explicitly mandate that the sensitivity analysis (FR-007) must also control for this variable in each re-run. If the sensitivity analysis drops the control, it drifts from the core research question. The spec must clarify that the sensitivity analysis re-runs the *full* hierarchical model (Step 1 + Step 2) for each threshold.
